headline: Stock Futures Slide With Markets on Edge Ahead of Big Tech Earnings
summary: Stocks were on course to open a touch lower on Monday, with investors seemingly reluctant to make any major moves ahead of a slew of Big Tech earnings and a Federal Reserve interest-rate decision.  S&P 500 futures slipped 0.1% and contracts tied to the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 were flat.  Both the S&P and the Nasdaq hit record closing highs at the end of last week, powered higher by Intel and other chip stocks.

content.title: Stock Futures Slide With Markets on Edge Ahead of Big Tech Earnings
content.summary: Stocks were on course to open a touch lower on Monday, with investors seemingly reluctant to make any major moves ahead of a slew of Big Tech earnings and a Federal Reserve interest-rate decision.  S&P 500 futures slipped 0.1% and contracts tied to the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 were flat.  Both the S&P and the Nasdaq hit record closing highs at the end of last week, powered higher by Intel and other chip stocks.

headline: Stocks Have Soared Ahead of Big Tech Earnings. There’s Little Room for Error.
summary: U.S. stocks are on an impressive run heading into this week’s slate of earnings, with an index of semiconductor names notching its longest winning streak on record, the Magnificent Seven tech giants rising nearly 20% since late March, and the broader  notching a series of record highs last week that pegs the benchmark well north of the 7000-point mark.  “We believe renewed focus on the AI space has been a clear driver of the recent lift in stocks, with compute deals, hyperscaler capital expenditure commitments, and early semiconductor earnings reinforcing confidence in the theme’s structural growth durability ahead of key Mag Seven earnings,” says Anthony Saglimbene, chief market strategist at Ameriprise.

content.title: Stocks Have Soared Ahead of Big Tech Earnings. There’s Little Room for Error.
content.summary: U.S. stocks are on an impressive run heading into this week’s slate of earnings, with an index of semiconductor names notching its longest winning streak on record, the Magnificent Seven tech giants rising nearly 20% since late March, and the broader  notching a series of record highs last week that pegs the benchmark well north of the 7000-point mark.  “We believe renewed focus on the AI space has been a clear driver of the recent lift in stocks, with compute deals, hyperscaler capital expenditure commitments, and early semiconductor earnings reinforcing confidence in the theme’s structural growth durability ahead of key Mag Seven earnings,” says Anthony Saglimbene, chief market strategist at Ameriprise.

headline: Stocks Are Wavering to Kick Off a Busy Week
summary: Stocks wavered on Monday to kick off the most packed week in months on Wall Street’s calendar.  “Despite bouts of volatility, for most of this month the showdown between geopolitical uncertainty and enthusiasm over AI-driven earnings growth has been a one-sided battle,” writes Chris Larkin, managing director, trading and investing at E*TRADE from Morgan Stanley.  “This week could show whether the bulls’ enthusiasm has been misplaced,” writes Larkin.

headline: OpenAI breaks off Microsoft exclusivity to free up path for Amazon, Google deals
summary: Microsoft will no longer have exclusive access to OpenAI's artificial intelligence models and ‌products, a significant change that will allow the ‌startup to sell its technology across rival cloud platforms including Amazon ​and Google.  Following the joint announcement, Microsoft shares slipped about 1% in premarket trading on Monday as some investors saw the shift as the company losing a valuable competitive advantage.  Microsoft ‌has in recent ⁠years emerged as a major player in the AI race, benefiting from its early bet ⁠on OpenAI that allowed it to quickly launch AI technology across its products.

content.title: OpenAI breaks off Microsoft exclusivity to free up path for Amazon, Google deals

headline: The Stock Market’s AI Rally Will Get a Test From Apple, Big Tech Earnings
summary: U.S. stocks are on an impressive run heading into this week’s slate of earnings, with an index of semiconductor names notching its longest winning streak on record, the Magnificent Seven tech giants rising nearly 20% since late March, and the broader  notching a series of record highs last week that pegs the benchmark well north of the 7000-point mark.  Communication services, information technology, and consumer discretionary, the three sectors that house the Mag Seven stocks and have driven around 60% of the market’s post-March rebound, are expected to contribute around 47% of S&P 500 earnings over the first quarter.

content.title: The Stock Market’s AI Rally Will Get a Test From Apple, Big Tech Earnings
content.summary: U.S. stocks are on an impressive run heading into this week’s slate of earnings, with an index of semiconductor names notching its longest winning streak on record, the Magnificent Seven tech giants rising nearly 20% since late March, and the broader  notching a series of record highs last week that pegs the benchmark well north of the 7000-point mark.  Communication services, information technology, and consumer discretionary, the three sectors that house the Mag Seven stocks and have driven around 60% of the market’s post-March rebound, are expected to contribute around 47% of S&P 500 earnings over the first quarter.

headline: Dow Slides, Nasdaq Futures Rise With Markets on Edge Ahead of Big Tech Earnings
summary: Stocks were wavering on Monday, with investors seemingly reluctant to make any major moves ahead of a slew of Big Tech earnings and a Federal Reserve interest-rate decision.  S&P 500 futures slipped 0.1% and contracts tied to the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 were up 0.1%.  Both the S&P and the Nasdaq hit record closing highs at the end of last week, powered higher by Intel and other chip stocks.

content.title: Dow Slides, Nasdaq Futures Rise With Markets on Edge Ahead of Big Tech Earnings
content.summary: Stocks were wavering on Monday, with investors seemingly reluctant to make any major moves ahead of a slew of Big Tech earnings and a Federal Reserve interest-rate decision.  S&P 500 futures slipped 0.1% and contracts tied to the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 were up 0.1%.  Both the S&P and the Nasdaq hit record closing highs at the end of last week, powered higher by Intel and other chip stocks.

headline: Elon Musk's trial against Sam Altman to reveal the ongoing power struggle for OpenAI
summary: The bitter legal fight between Elon Musk and the leading artificial intelligence firm, OpenAI, led by Sam Altman, may come down to a few pages in one executive's personal diary.  "This is the only chance we have to get out from Elon," wrote Greg Brockman, OpenAI's president ‌and a co-founder, in the fall of 2017.  Brockman's diary entry is part of the thousands of pages of internal documents revealed in court since Musk, one ‌of the original co-founders of OpenAI, sued the company, its chief executive Altman and Brockman in 2024.

content.title: Elon Musk's trial against Sam Altman to reveal the ongoing power struggle for OpenAI
content.summary: The bitter legal fight between Elon Musk and the leading artificial intelligence firm, OpenAI, led by Sam Altman, may come down to a few pages in one executive's personal diary.  "This is the only chance we have to get out from Elon," wrote Greg Brockman, OpenAI's president ‌and a co-founder, in the fall of 2017.  Brockman's diary entry is part of the thousands of pages of internal documents revealed in court since Musk, one ‌of the original co-founders of OpenAI, sued the company, its chief executive Altman and Brockman in 2024.

headline: Nvidia could be undervalued by at least 30%: BofA analyst on Mag 7
summary: While five of the Magnificent Seven companies are releasing earnings results this week — Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN), Apple (AAPL), Meta Platforms (META), and Microsoft (MSFT) — the last one to report this earnings season is chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) on May 20. BofA Securities senior semiconductor analyst Vivek Arya comes on Market Catalysts to share how much he believes Nvidia to be undervalued. Nvidia's market cap crossed back above $5 trillion last week.

BofA Securities senior semiconductor analyst Vivek Arya comes on Market Catalysts to share how much he believes Nvidia to be undervalued.

Nvidia's market cap crossed back above $5 trillion last week.

headline: Microsoft facing UK antitrust lawsuit from Slack over Teams 'bundling'
summary: Salesforce and its workspace messaging app Slack are suing ‌Microsoft at London's High Court over ‌alleged anticompetitive practices relating to its Teams app.  Slack Technologies ​LLC and related companies filed the lawsuit on April 23, which a Slack spokesperson said was "because Microsoft's practices harmed competition, using ‌tying and bundling ⁠of Teams to limit customer choice".  Microsoft did not immediately respond to ⁠a request for comment.

content.title: Microsoft facing UK antitrust lawsuit from Slack over Teams 'bundling'
content.summary: Salesforce and its workspace messaging app Slack are suing ‌Microsoft at London's High Court over ‌alleged anticompetitive practices relating to its Teams app.  Slack Technologies ​LLC and related companies filed the lawsuit on April 23, which a Slack spokesperson said was "because Microsoft's practices harmed competition, using ‌tying and bundling ⁠of Teams to limit customer choice".  Microsoft did not immediately respond to ⁠a request for comment.

headline: Microsoft cuts OpenAI revenue share in a fresh step to loosen their AI alliance
summary: Microsoft said Monday it will no longer pay a share of its revenue to ChatGPT maker OpenAI, the latest move to untether a close partnership that helped unleash an artificial intelligence boom.  OpenAI relied exclusively on Microsoft's investments in cloud computing services to build the technology that helped make ChatGPT a household name.  Microsoft, in turn, relied on OpenAI's technology to build its own AI assistant Copilot.

content.title: Microsoft cuts OpenAI revenue share in a fresh step to loosen their AI alliance
content.summary: Microsoft said Monday it will no longer pay a share of its revenue to ChatGPT maker OpenAI, the latest move to untether a close partnership that helped unleash an artificial intelligence boom.  OpenAI relied exclusively on Microsoft's investments in cloud computing services to build the technology that helped make ChatGPT a household name.  Microsoft, in turn, relied on OpenAI's technology to build its own AI assistant Copilot.

headline: At $265: How Amazon Stock Is Getting Rerated
summary: Amazon (AMZN) stock recently crossed its 52-week high to trade near $265, posting a 16% year-to-date gain. At that level, the 34x forward multiple leads many retail investors to conclude the stock has become expensive, typically by comparing it to its four-year historical forward average of roughly 29x. That comparison is the wrong benchmark for where Amazon stands today. The premium to its historical average is not a warning sign but a re-rating, as the market correctly prices in Amazon Web Ser

headline: Elon Musk set to face off against Sam Altman in OpenAI trial
summary: Tesla (TSLA) and SpaceX (SPAX.PVT) CEO Elon Musk is set to meet OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) CEO Sam Altman in court this week over an alleged breach of the private AI developer's non-profit mission. Musk is also a co-founder of OpenAI. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland break down the details of the court case.

Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland break down the details of the court case.

headline: Stocks Are Slipping to Kick Off a Busy Week
summary: Stocks wavered on Monday to kick off the most packed week in months on Wall Street’s calendar.  “Despite bouts of volatility, for most of this month the showdown between geopolitical uncertainty and enthusiasm over AI-driven earnings growth has been a one-sided battle,” writes Chris Larkin, managing director, trading and investing at E*TRADE from Morgan Stanley.  “This week could show whether the bulls’ enthusiasm has been misplaced,” writes Larkin.
